response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack software developer with strengths in web technologies, TypeScript/Node.js, and developer tooling, but has no demonstrable experience in any of the core competencies this role demands: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, hardware-software co-design, GPU workload analysis, or deep neural network performance optimization. His closest hardware-adjacent experience is a brief stint writing C/C++ components for a measurement IDE (NI LabWindows) and a bachelor thesis involving audio synthesis — neither of which translates to silicon-level performance architecture. Recent AI involvement is limited to MCP framework tooling, which is application-layer work entirely disconnected from accelerator hardware optimization.
strengths: Long career (15+ years) demonstrating sustained technical growth and breadth across systems, languages, and domains
Low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic with async I/O and high-throughput microservices shows systems-level thinking
C/C++ background from NI and embedded/robotics work at Altom provides a thin foothold in compiled, performance-sensitive code
concerns: Zero SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training/inference optimization experience whatsoever
Entire career trajectory is web and application software; there is no credible pivot path to principal-level hardware performance architecture without significant re-skilling

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture background
No AI accelerator or GPU workload analysis experience
Breadth of systems experience (low-latency, embedded, C/C++)
